Architect Questions: Why is the organization building this system? What problem are we solving?
Who are the humans/users interacting with the system? Analysts, engineers, applications?
What value or business yield will this system produce?
What is the primary goal of the system or platform?
What outcomes must be achieved in the next 6–12 months?
Which teams, services, or domains are involved?
What limits exist (budget, regulation, legacy systems)?
What security or compliance requirements exist?
What uptime and reliability expectations exist?
What core capabilities must the system support?
What performance or scale requirements exist?
From which sources does the data originate?
How will data be loaded or ingested?
How will the platform organize data and compute?
What workloads will consume the platform?
How will pipelines ingest and process data?
How will analytics and BI models be structured?
How will ML models be trained and deployed?
What AI or application workloads will run?
How flexible should the architecture be?
Are we optimizing for accuracy or speed?
Can we simplify the architecture to reduce complexity?
What latency requirements exist?
How will the system remain reliable and fault tolerant?
How will we monitor and observe system behavior?
How will networking and security boundaries be enforced?
Will this improve operational workflows?
Will the platform deliver faster insights?
Will this enable ML, AI, automation, or new products?
What architecture strategy are we recommending?
What technology or platform enables the strategy?
What measurable outcomes will the business see?
What ROI or business return will this generate?
How does this support long-term strategy?
